cms-4: Offensive testing that finds the gaps before an attacker does.
cms-6: Managed detection and response watching your estate around the clock.
cms-8: Cloud, data and identity controls that hold under pressure.
cms-10: Risk, compliance and critical systems, mapped to your business.
cms-11: Thirty capabilities, organised into four dimensions.
cms-21: A controlled attack on your own systems, run by people who do this for a living. We find the path in, prove it, and hand you a fix, before someone less friendly finds it first.
cms-24: What a 4D penetration test actually delivers.
cms-25: Plenty of tools can list vulnerabilities. A penetration test answers a harder question: can someone chain those weaknesses together to reach something that matters? We test the way a real attacker operates, then translate what we find into decisions your team can act on.
cms-26: Every engagement is scoped to a goal you care about, reaching customer data, moving from a laptop to the domain, or pivoting from IT into the plant floor. We work safely, communicate as we go, and never leave you guessing.
cms-27: You get a ranked report tied to business impact, a walkthrough with your team, and a free retest once you have remediated. Not a PDF that gathers dust.
cms-29: Scope and rules of engagement agreed in writing, so everyone knows the boundaries.
cms-30: Reconnaissance and mapping of your real, current attack surface.
cms-31: Exploitation and privilege escalation, proving impact rather than guessing at it.
cms-32: Clear reporting, ranked by risk, with a walkthrough and remediation guidance.
cms-33: A retest to confirm the fixes actually closed the door.
cms-35: Pick a focused test or a full-scope red team. We will help you choose based on your risk, not our rate card.
cms-38: Test the perimeter and what an attacker could do once inside.
cms-41: Business logic, auth and injection flaws in the apps you ship.
cms-44: Real-world attack paths through AWS, Azure and Google Cloud.
cms-47: Phishing and pretext attacks that test people and process.
cms-50: The offices, networks and doors that software cannot see.
cms-53: A goal-based, multi-vector operation against your live defenses.
cms-54: Ready to be tested on purpose?
cms-55: Tell us what you want us to try to reach. We will scope a penetration test that proves where you stand.
cms-59: Multi-dimensional security built around your operational goals, from offensive testing to governance.
cms-75: Goal-driven penetration testing from 4D Security: network, web and API, cloud, wireless, social engineering and full red team. Findings ranked by business risk.
